treatment_information.treatment_response: Significantly agitated and combative upon admission, but sedation improved condition.
Part of treatment for ST elevation, no complications noted.
Administered to manage angina through vasodilation.
Part of treatment for coronary vasospasm, effective during the monitoring phase.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient admitted after being found agitated; initial vitals showed significant tachycardia and hypertension.
Patient received 10 mg IM haldol and 6 mg IM ativan for agitation.
EKG revealed ST elevations in V2 and V3.
Patient received treatment with aspirin, heparin, nitroglycerin, and diltiazem.
Patient stabilized with improved vitals prior to ICU transfer.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow up with nurse practitioner for EKG reassessment.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Patient's EKG changes resolved without evidence of permanent damage.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Patient reported clear mental status and is independent in activity.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient was informed of the resolution of EKG changes and expressed interest in ongoing therapy for drug and alcohol use.
